commitMessage:
Bug 574610 - [Quickfix] Create method in supertype adds extra @Override if it's already present

Change-Id: I06e15b5bb844a76b7b02720a31dde9a3985d8a61
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/182712
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: This commit fixes a bug within an existing JDT UI quick-fix. It's a bug fix for internal JDT behavior, not a new user-facing transformation pattern.
languageChangeNeeded: The core of this bug fix involves making a method call conditional (e.g., `if (fAddOverrideAnnotation) { addOverrideAnnotation(document); }`). The current TriggerPattern DSL does not support inserting arbitrary control flow (like `if` statements) into replacement patterns at the expression rewrite level. It would require a more powerful statement-level transformation capability.
dslRuleAfterChange: Not applicable, requires fundamental DSL extension.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in JDT's 'Create method in supertype' quick fix, preventing it from adding redundant `@Override` annotations. The fix introduces a new boolean parameter and field to control annotation generation. While the propagation of this boolean parameter through method and constructor calls could be partially captured by DSL rules (argument addition), the crucial step of conditionally executing `addOverrideAnnotation()` using an `if` statement in the replacement is not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL. Therefore, the core logic of the bug fix cannot be implemented with the existing DSL capabilities, classifying it as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 574703: [17][quick fix][regression] NPE on hover when permitted type
is not editable

Change-Id: I07f105bc7ea485832eab5f9f3d41faeea2fc8674
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/182837
Tested-by: Noopur Gupta <noopur_gupta@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-by: Noopur Gupta <noopur_gupta@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: The change directly addresses a NullPointerException by adding a null check.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs support for inserting new control flow statements (e.g., if blocks with return statements) before or around existing code, rather than just replacing an expression or a single statement.
summary: This commit fixes a NullPointerException by adding a null check: `if (compilationUnit == null) { return; }`. While preventing NPEs is a common and highly reusable null-safety pattern, the current TriggerPattern DSL does not support inserting new `if` statements or other control flow blocks as part of a replacement rule. The DSL primarily focuses on matching and replacing existing expressions or statements, not injecting new structural code elements before or around matched patterns. Therefore, this transformation is classified as RED, indicating it cannot be implemented with the current DSL capabilities.

Bug 574860: [17][quick fix][sealed] declare sealed class as super class
of permitted types

categoryReason: This commit fixes an internal bug in JDT's quick-fix logic for Java 17 Sealed Classes, preventing incorrect application for classes vs. interfaces.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires the DSL to support generating new local variable declarations and complex control flow statements (if-else, switch cases) in the replacement patterns.
summary: This commit refactors an internal JDT quick fix for Java 17 Sealed Classes. It replaces a direct super-interface insertion with conditional logic (if-else) to correctly handle both sealed interfaces and sealed classes by choosing between `SUPER_INTERFACE_TYPES_PROPERTY` and `SUPERCLASS_TYPE_PROPERTY`. It also conditionally formats the proposal label. This involves introducing new local variables and control flow statements, which is a structural code change not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Bug 570604 - New Java project: enable '--release' option for Java 7+

When creating a Java project, the '--release' option should be enabled
if the compiler compliance level is 1.7 or higher; otherwise it should
be disabled.

Change-Id: I00f575a82cb1ee31639774e1bbc554f473fdc5cb
Signed-off-by: Holger Voormann <eclipse@voormann.de>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/175347
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: This commit evolves internal JDT API by adding new logic for configuring `--release` option during new Java project creation.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires the DSL to support inserting entirely new blocks of code, including new local variable declarations and method invocations, rather than just transforming existing expressions or statements.
summary: This commit adds new logic to `BuildPathSupport.java` to enable the `--release` option when creating a new Java project, based on the compiler compliance level being Java 7 or higher. This involves inserting new local variable declarations and conditional logic (a ternary expression), which is a structural code addition not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Bug 574400 - ModifyDialog$FocusManager leaks Font

- change ModifyDialog.FocusManager.changeFont() method to
  add DisposeListener to the control to dispose of the new
  Font created

Change-Id: Ia0d0dd0c97604803692522d78b008e12c0a7fb30
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/182479
Reviewed-by: Andrey Loskutov <loskutov@gmx.de>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: This pattern addresses a specific resource leak (SWT Font not disposed) in UI code, which is a specialized form of resource management common in Eclipse development.
languageChangeNeeded:
The DSL would need capabilities to:
1.  Transform a single expression statement into a block of multiple statements.
2.  Introduce new local variable declarations within the replacement block.
3.  Insert new method calls (e.g., `addDisposeListener`) before or after the original expression's context.
These are advanced structural transformations beyond the current expression-based rewrite capabilities.
dslRuleAfterChange:
A hypothetical DSL rule might look like:

`@id: swt.font.dispose-on-control-dispose`
`@severity: error`
`$control.setFont(new org.eclipse.swt.graphics.Font($display, $fontData));`
`=> {`
`    org.eclipse.swt.graphics.Font __font = new org.eclipse.swt.graphics.Font($display, $fontData);`
`    $control.addDisposeListener(e -> __font.dispose());`
`    $control.setFont(__font);`
`}`
`;;`

This syntax is not supported by the current DSL.
summary: This commit fixes a font resource leak in an Eclipse UI dialog. It transforms a single `control.setFont(new Font(...))` line into multiple statements: declaring a local `Font` variable, adding a `DisposeListener` to the `control` (using a Java 8 lambda) to dispose of the font, and then setting the font. This is a crucial bug fix for memory management. However, the transformation involves inserting multiple statements, including local variable declarations and a listener registration, which is not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL. This makes it a RED transformation.

categoryReason: This pattern modifies basic control flow structures to potentially improve code style or optimize a specific execution path.
languageChangeNeeded: DSL needs advanced control-flow and data-flow analysis to determine if a `while` loop's condition is initially always true and passive, allowing safe conversion to `do-while`.
summary: This commit introduces a new Java-based cleanup to convert `while` loops to `do-while` loops under specific semantic conditions (initial condition always true and passive). This requires advanced control-flow and data-flow analysis, which is beyond the current TriggerPattern DSL's capabilities, classifying it as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 575129 - [cleanup] "Optimization" tab should be "Performance" tab

The "Optimization" tab in the Cleanup settings should be named
"Performance" tab. It is ambiguous. You can also optimize the size of
your source code.

categoryReason: This is an internal refactoring to improve naming conventions within the JDT UI codebase.
summary: This commit renames UI labels and internal class references from "Optimization" to "Performance" within the JDT cleanup preferences. This is a mechanical batch renaming of static field accesses and FQCNs. Two GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rules using a `<!map>` directive can automate these replacements, suitable for `jdt-internal-refactoring.sandbox-hint`.

commitMessage:
Bug 574640 - New Java Project dialog: show scrollbars when required

In the "New Java Project" dialog/wizard, on the first page, show
scrollbars when required instead of skipping the content at the bottom.
Otherwise, when the dialog height is too small, the "Create
module-info.java file" checkbox and any existing infos and warnings
displayed below the checkbox may be hidden and thus become inaccessible.

Change-Id: I1423b621f339f605ac0c56a655bbda09da9230a3
Signed-off-by: Holger Voormann <eclipse@voormann.de>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/182740
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
irrelevantReason: The commit involves extensive structural UI refactoring, including introducing new SWT components (ScrolledComposite), adding event listeners (ControlAdapter), overriding UI lifecycle methods (createControl, setVisible), and introducing new private helper methods. These changes are highly specific to Eclipse SWT UI architecture and cannot be generalized into reusable TriggerPattern DSL rules.
categoryReason: The transformation focuses on improving the user interface and layout of an Eclipse dialog, involving specific SWT API usage and structural changes to UI components.
summary: This commit addresses Bug 574640 by refactoring the 'New Java Project' dialog to display scrollbars when content overflows. This involves significant structural changes to the UI, including wrapping the main composite in a `ScrolledComposite`, adjusting `GridData` layouts, adding `ControlAdapter` listeners, and introducing private helper methods to manage visibility and size updates. These changes are highly specific to the Eclipse SWT UI framework and the particular dialog's architecture. They cannot be generalized into reusable, pattern-based TriggerPattern DSL rules, as the DSL is not designed for complex UI composition, event handling, or structural refactorings involving multiple new methods and class hierarchies. Therefore, the commit is classified as NOT_APPLICABLE for DSL mining.

commitMessage:
Bug 573126 - Use defaultprofile id

Change-Id: I976b8ea1f2927639bd8b12fa12f749eddd43a7cd
Signed-off-by: Carsten Hammer <carsten.hammer@t-online.de>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/179765
Reviewed-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: The change fixes a functional bug by ensuring a method's return value is captured.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL currently does not support transforming an arbitrary expression statement (e.g., `methodCall();`) into an assignment statement (e.g., `variable = methodCall();`). This would require a more advanced AST transformation capability to identify ignored return values and assign them to specific local variables based on control flow analysis.
summary: This commit fixes a bug where the return value of `fProfileManager.getDefaultProfile().getID()` was being discarded, leading to `id` remaining null when it shouldn't. The change assigns the method's result to the `id` variable. This transforms a standalone method call statement into an assignment statement. This kind of structural change, where an expression's result needs to be assigned to a variable, is not directly supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL's expression-to-expression or statement-to-statement rewrite rules. It requires more advanced AST manipulation to add the assignment operation and determine the target variable, hence it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 575026 - Provide support for Refactor > Rename of Modules

Change-Id: I527acb5f3f6197c48c87f9bb4763616e3b38fa8c
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/183358
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: The changes modernize internal Eclipse JDT UI APIs to support new features (module renaming) by adding arguments to existing method calls.
summary: This commit adds support for renaming Java modules in JDT UI. This involves modifying several internal JDT UI method calls related to rename actions and availability checks. The key patterns are the addition of a `boolean isTextSelection` argument to `RefactoringAvailabilityTester.isRenameElementAvailable()` and to `RenameJavaElementAction.doRun()`. The argument is set to `true` in some contexts (e.g., initial UI checks, quick assists) and passed from a method parameter `isTextSelection` in others (e.g., core execution logic). These are direct 1:1 method call argument additions, which are fully expressible as multiple TriggerPattern DSL rules. The `inClass` and `inMethod` guards ensure contextual application. Other changes involving new class creations (e.g., `RenameModuleWizard`), new NLS messages, or constant declarations are architectural and not pattern-based code transformations. The rules are applicable for Java 9+ due to the context of module features.

commitMessage:
Bug 522218 - Paste String auto escape toggle

- add new raw paste functionality to context menu that does not
  honor smart insert
- add new RAW_PASTE constant to ClipboardOperationAction
- add new logic in ClipboardOperationAction.doOperation() to
  do a paste with smart insert off
- also modify ClipboardOperationAction.update() to calculate
  canDoOperation() for PASTE when action is RAW_PASTE
- modify JavaEditor to create a special ClipboardOperationAction
  for raw paste
- modify BasicJavaEditorActionContributor likewise to support
  the new raw paste action
- add appropriate messages, command, and action

Change-Id: Ia77b8cd494b29595a5d5cb91c5de2f629dd7e1ce
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/183535
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The commit introduces a new UI feature and involves complex structural changes in an internal UI component.
languageChangeNeeded: The core of this feature involves adding an `if` statement with a block of multiple new statements (method calls to `setInsertMode`, `doOperation`), which is not supported by the current DSL for arbitrary code block insertions. The DSL only supports multiline replacements for simple continuation lines within an expression.
summary: This commit introduces a 'Raw Paste' functionality to the Java editor, which allows pasting text without honoring smart insert settings. The changes include the declaration of a new internal `RAW_PASTE` constant, new NLS messages, and modifications to `ClipboardOperationAction`. Specifically, `ClipboardOperationAction.update()` is modified to use a ternary operator for `canDoOperation()` based on `fOperationCode`, and `ClipboardOperationAction.internalDoOperation()` is modified to add an `if (fOperationCode == RAW_PASTE)` block. This `if` block contains multiple statements, including calls to `setInsertMode()` and `doOperation()`, and is a complex structural change that cannot be expressed as a simple pattern-based rewrite in the current TriggerPattern DSL. While the boolean expression modification is technically feasible, the core new functionality relies on unsupported structural changes, making the commit classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 575507 - In the first page of New Java Project dialog, scroll bar
doesn't scroll on tab event

Change-Id: Idc07cd12c0daa49ac7b1b4842bcd3069822f9d00
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/184233
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Noopur Gupta <noopur_gupta@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-by: Noopur Gupta <noopur_gupta@in.ibm.com>
summary: This commit fixes a UI bug where the scroll bar in the 'New Java Project' dialog doesn't scroll on a tab event. It introduces a call to `scroll.setShowFocusedControl(true)` on an `org.eclipse.swt.custom.ScrolledComposite` instance. This is a direct API call insertion that enhances UI behavior. The pattern is a direct, semantically safe addition of a method call after an existing one, fully supported by the TriggerPattern DSL's multiline replacement feature. The rule is guarded by `instanceof` and `inClass` to ensure it applies to the correct UI component and context, making it a reusable fix for similar SWT UI components. The `sourceVersion` is 8 as SWT is compatible with Java 8 baseline.

commitMessage:
Bug 513687 - Double-click includes escape chars

Provide a JavaStringDoubleClickStrategy that treats valid Java
character escapes as "words" to be selected on double click. Install
it for the string, character, and text block document partitions in
the JavaSourceViewerConfiguration.

The strategy depends on the source version because \s is a valid escape
only as of Java 15.

Change-Id: I2cfe61ceb18d5f6bb9e86d7024954afdbc47497b
Signed-off-by: Thomas Wolf <thomas.wolf@paranor.ch>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/184277
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
irrelevantReason: The commit introduces a new UI feature and integrates it into existing JDT UI infrastructure. It's about implementing new logic for a double-click strategy for string literals and configuring it based on Java source version, rather than a general code transformation pattern for user Java code.
categoryReason: The changes are specific to the Eclipse JDT UI's behavior, not general Java code refactoring.
summary: This commit implements a new JavaStringDoubleClickStrategy for the JDT UI, allowing double-click selection of escape characters in string literals. It involves adding a new class and integrating it into JavaSourceViewerConfiguration, including logic to adapt to source version changes (e.g., for '\s' escape in Java 15+). This is a feature implementation and UI integration, not a generalizable code transformation pattern for user code, hence it's not applicable for DSL mining.

commitMessage:
Bug 575517 - Generate toString should work for record classes too

Change-Id: I8e13809592d19cd9ea0c5df584c20433d2ff8f5d
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/184245
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Andrey Loskutov <loskutov@gmx.de>
categoryReason: The change modifies internal JDT UI code to support a new Java language feature (records) by adding control flow logic, which is an internal refactoring.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support injecting new control flow statements (like if-else blocks) into existing methods. It primarily supports expression-level or simple statement replacements. To implement this, the DSL would need to allow richer AST manipulation, such as inserting if-else statements or entire block structures.
summary: This commit fixes Bug 575517 by updating the internal JDT UI action `GenerateToStringAction` to correctly handle Java 16 `record` classes. The change involves adding an `if-else` statement to dynamically select between `IType.getRecordComponents()` and `IType.getFields()` for `record` types versus regular classes. This is a structural modification to add new control flow, which is not directly supported by the TriggerPattern DSL's expression-level or simple statement replacement capabilities. Therefore, it is classified as RED. The `sourceVersion` is 16 because `record` classes were introduced in Java 16.

commitMessage:
Bug 575907 - fixed DocumentCharacterIterator#toString

A CharSequence has to return its content in toString().

Change-Id: I070af386ee61bb6fc4193da4c08c1a541b39b51b
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/185264
Reviewed-by: Jörg Kubitz <jkubitz-eclipse@gmx.de>
Reviewed-by: Andrey Loskutov <loskutov@gmx.de>
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: The change explicitly aims to fulfill the contract of CharSequence's toString() method, returning content rather than object identity.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support identifying classes that implement a specific interface (e.g., `CharSequence`) and then generating a new method declaration (e.g., `toString()`) with a body derived from the class's internal structure (e.g., `fDocument.get().substring(...)`). This would require more advanced context-aware code generation capabilities beyond simple pattern matching and replacement.
summary: This commit adds a specific `toString()` method implementation to `DocumentCharacterIterator` to ensure it correctly returns its content, fulfilling the `CharSequence` contract. The implementation directly references internal fields (`fDocument`, `getBeginIndex`, `getEndIndex`), making it highly specific. The TriggerPattern DSL does not support inserting entire new method declarations with complex, context-dependent bodies, so this transformation is classified as RED.

categoryReason: This is a typical modernization for new Java language features (JEP 378 Text Blocks).
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need advanced AST traversal and rewrite capabilities to identify complex string concatenation patterns, build a new Text Block literal, and correctly handle embedded expressions and escaping. This is a highly structural transformation that is not a simple expression-to-expression replacement.
summary: This commit introduces a new JDT cleanup to convert string concatenations to Java 15 Text Blocks. This transformation requires complex AST analysis and structural rewriting to correctly identify suitable concatenations, build a new Text Block literal, and handle embedded expressions. This level of transformation is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL's expression-based rewrite rules, making it a RED classification.

summary: This commit fixes a potential NullPointerException in JDT's internal `QuickAssistProcessor` by adding a null check before invoking `isSynthetic()` on `IMethodBinding`. This is a direct 1:1 expression replacement within a boolean condition, improving the robustness of internal JDT logic. It is fully expressible as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule, guarded by `inClass` and `inMethod` for precise application.

commitMessage:
Bug 575612 - "Operand factorization" may allow active expression

If an active expression is at the very start or at the very end of the
operation and is not the duplicate expression, so we can factorize the
expression.

Given:
((i++ > 0) && duplicateExpression) || (duplicateExpression &&
elseExpression)

When:
Clean up the code enabling "Replace (X && Y) || (X && Z) by (X && (Y ||
Z))"...

Then:
(((i++ > 0) || elseExpression) && duplicateExpression)

Change-Id: Idbf9ebbd0799f72b324d0cc63ed14d874c31b281
Signed-off-by: Fabrice Tiercelin <fabrice.tiercelin@yahoo.fr>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/184397
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: This commit modifies the internal logic of an existing cleanup to improve its correctness and safety under various expression types and evaluation rules.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need advanced capabilities for complex AST subtree matching and variable extraction in nested logical expressions, semantic analysis of side effects and operator short-circuiting, and conditional rewriting based on these complex semantic properties. This is beyond current DSL capabilities.
summary: This commit enhances the `OperandFactorizationCleanUp` by refining its logic for handling active expressions and operator types. The complexity of safely factorizing operands, which involves analyzing side effects, operator precedence, and short-circuiting behavior, makes this transformation unsuitable for the current TriggerPattern DSL. It's an internal bug fix/enhancement to an existing Java-based cleanup, classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 576254 - Improve handling of enumerations on Javadoc hover.

- The Javadoc view logic assumes an enumeration is generally a variable
  with some constant that can be displayed on hover. However,
  enumerations can be declared as anonymous classes, where each element
  can also override the shared methods leading to class casting issues

Change-Id: I101fc93ca431858e96f2e4a3baed39f06655028f
Signed-off-by: Roland Grunberg <rgrunber@redhat.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/185812
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: This fixes a ClassCastException in JDT's internal Javadoc view logic.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need capabilities to recognize and refactor variable declarations with immediate casts, and to restructure `if` statements by pushing down conditions and removing intermediate variables. This is a multi-statement structural transformation not supported by the current DSL.
summary: This commit fixes a ClassCastException in JDT's internal Javadoc view by replacing an unconditional cast and a separate null check with an `instanceof` check and a conditional cast. This involves restructuring a variable declaration and an `if` statement, which is a complex structural change not directly supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL's expression-based rewrite rules, thus classified as RED.

categoryReason: This change targets an internal Eclipse JDT UI constant, impacting its behavior for performance/responsiveness. It's specific to JDT's internal API rather than a general Java or Eclipse API modernization.
summary: This commit addresses a UI freeze by changing an internal JDT constant from `SharedASTProviderCore.WAIT_YES` to `SharedASTProviderCore.WAIT_NO`. This modifies the behavior of AST retrieval to be non-blocking, improving UI responsiveness. This is a direct 1:1 constant replacement, fully implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. Other changes involve adding a method overload for `findEnumConstructorTarget` and minor code formatting within an `if` condition, which are not directly expressible as pattern-based DSL transformations.

commitMessage:
Bug 576432 - [16] [quickfix] Provide quick fix to create a new Record
when suggesting new types

Change-Id: I4eb9b6d42314399c2b727d591e231321a184219d
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/186134
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: This commit adds a new quick fix within Eclipse JDT UI's internal correction processor, specifically for creating Record types.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL currently only supports pattern-matching and replacement of existing code. It does not support adding new if-blocks, new case statements in switch, or defining complex custom helper methods and then integrating their logic into new code insertions. This commit adds a new quick fix, which is a structural addition of new UI and logic, rather than a transformation of existing code.
summary: This commit adds a new quick fix proposal to create a new Record type, applicable when suggesting new types in JDT UI for Java 16+ projects. This involves structurally adding new conditional logic, new `NewCUUsingWizardProposal` instantiation, and new `case` statements for `K_RECORD` in internal wizard pages. The transformation is a feature addition rather than a modification of existing code patterns, and its complexity, including the introduction of a custom helper method (`canUseRecord`) and conditional code generation, falls outside the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL which is focused on pattern matching and replacement.
